title: 🚛 Professional Driver / Refresher Course
description: Advanced training for experienced drivers. Comprehensive refresher course covering defensive driving, advanced techniques, fleet management, and professional conduct.

units.title: Advanced Defensive Driving
units.content:
# Advanced Defensive Driving

## Core Principles for Professional Drivers

### The Smith System (5 Keys)
1. **Aim High in Steering** - Look 15-20 seconds ahead
2. **Get the Big Picture** - Maintain 360-degree awareness
3. **Keep Your Eyes Moving** - Scan, don't stare
4. **Leave Yourself an Out** - Always have escape route
5. **Make Sure They See You** - Use signals, lights, horn

### Space Management
- **Following Distance**: 4 seconds minimum (increase to 6-8 seconds in adverse conditions)
- **Front Space**: Always leave 2 car lengths in front
- **Side Space**: Position to avoid blind spots
- **Rear Space**: Monitor following traffic, adjust speed
- **Escape Routes**: Always identify at least one escape path

### Hazard Perception
- Scan 12-15 seconds ahead for potential hazards
- Identify developing hazards (vehicles merging, brake lights, pedestrians)
- Categorize hazards by risk level
- Plan responses before hazard becomes critical
- Always have Plan B

### Intersection Management
- Cover brake when approaching all intersections
- Count 1-2-3 after light turns green before proceeding
- Look left-right-left before entering
- Watch for red-light runners
- Be extra cautious of unprotected left turns

### Night Driving for Professionals
- Reduce speed by 10-15 km/h
- Increase following distance to 6 seconds
- Clean all windows and mirrors
- Adjust mirrors to reduce glare
- Take breaks every 2 hours to combat fatigue
- Never out-drive your headlights

### Urban vs Rural Driving

**Urban Driving:**
- Anticipate pedestrian movement
- Watch for opening car doors
- Be aware of delivery vehicles
- Expect sudden stops
- Use engine braking

**Rural Driving:**
- Watch for animals on road
- Be cautious on narrow roads
- Anticipate farm equipment
- Watch for loose gravel
- Use high beams when safe
key_points: Smith System: Aim high, get big picture, keep eyes moving, leave an out, make sure they see you
Maintain 4-second minimum following distance (6-8 seconds in bad weather)
Cover brake at all intersections, look left-right-left
Never out-drive your headlights at night
Always identify at least one escape route

units.title: Skid Control & Recovery - Advanced
units.content:
# Skid Control & Recovery - Advanced

## Understanding Vehicle Dynamics

### Weight Transfer
- Acceleration transfers weight to rear
- Braking transfers weight to front
- Turning transfers weight to outside wheels
- Combined inputs multiply effects
- Smooth inputs maintain balance

### Traction Circle Concept
- Tyres have 100% available traction
- Braking uses traction
- Turning uses traction
- Accelerating uses traction
- Combined demands exceed available traction = skid

## Types of Skids - Advanced Recognition

### Front Wheel Skid (Understeer)
**Causes:**
- Entering corner too fast
- Braking while turning
- Front tyre under-inflation
- Wet or icy conditions

**Recovery:**
1. Immediately release brakes
2. Gradually release steering
3. Allow front tyres to regain grip
4. Re-apply steering gently
5. Reduce throttle

### Rear Wheel Skid (Oversteer)
**Causes:**
- Too much throttle in corner
- Lifting off throttle abruptly
- Rear tyre over-inflation
- Slippery road surface

**Recovery:**
1. Steer into the skid (direction rear is sliding)
2. Gently reduce throttle (don't completely lift)
3. Look where you want to go
4. Catch the skid with opposite steering
5. Be ready for counter-skid

### Braking Skid (Locked Wheels)
**Causes:**
- Panic braking
- Brake system imbalance
- ABS failure (if equipped)
- Low-traction surface

**Recovery:**
1. Release brake pressure
2. Pump brakes (without ABS)
3. Maintain steering input
4. Re-apply brakes smoothly
5. Use engine braking

## Aquaplaning - Detailed

### Prevention
- Reduce speed in rain (50 km/h or less)
- Avoid standing water
- Maintain proper tyre tread (min 3mm)
- Proper tyre pressure
- Avoid cruise control in rain

### Recognition
- Sudden light steering feel
- Engine revs increase without acceleration
- Steering becomes unresponsive
- Rear may feel loose
- Speedometer continues climbing

### Recovery
1. Do NOT brake or turn sharply
2. Gently ease off accelerator
3. Keep steering straight
4. Wait for tyres to contact road
5. Gently brake after recovery

## Advanced Skid Prevention

### Threshold Braking
- Brake at maximum without locking
- Feel for brake pedal vibration (ABS activation point)
- Maintain steering control
- Practice in safe area
- Essential for non-ABS vehicles

### Trail Braking
- Maintain light brake pressure into corner
- Transfer weight to front for grip
- Release brake as you add steering
- Advanced technique - practice required
- Not for public roads in poor conditions

### Heel-Toe Downshifting
- Match engine revs to road speed
- Smooth downshifts prevent rear lock-up
- Maintains vehicle balance
- Professional technique
- Reduces driveline shock
key_points: Understeer (front skid): release brakes, reduce steering input
Oversteer (rear skid): steer into skid, gently reduce throttle
Aquaplaning: ease off accelerator, keep steering straight
Threshold braking: maximum braking without locking wheels
Traction circle: don't exceed tyre's available grip

units.title: Adverse Weather & Emergency Conditions
units.content:
# Adverse Weather & Emergency Conditions

## Heavy Rain Driving

### Preparation
- Check wipers before journey
- Ensure tyres have adequate tread (min 3mm)
- Test defroster operation
- Check all lights
- Have emergency kit accessible

### Driving Techniques
- Reduce speed by 20-30 km/h
- Double following distance (8 seconds)
- Use headlights (not just daytime running lights)
- Avoid cruise control
- Stay in tyre tracks of vehicle ahead
- Brake earlier and gentler
- Watch for hydroplaning signs

### Puddle Management
- Avoid if possible (may hide pothole)
- If unavoidable, slow before puddle
- Release brakes as you enter
- Keep steering straight
- Lightly pump brakes after to dry

## Fog Driving

### Preparation
- Clean all windows inside and out
- Ensure defroster works
- Check fog lights if equipped
- Know route before departing
- Allow extra travel time

### Driving Techniques
- Reduce speed significantly (40 km/h or less)
- Use low beam headlights (high beams reflect back)
- Use fog lights if available
- Follow road lines, not tail lights
- Avoid passing or changing lanes
- Roll down window at intersections to listen
- If visibility near zero, pull off completely

### Emergency Fog Procedures
- Don't stop in travel lanes
- If must stop, pull far off road
- Turn off headlights (attract other drivers)
- Use parking lights only
- Stay in vehicle with seatbelt fastened

## Extreme Heat Driving

### Vehicle Preparation
- Check coolant levels
- Inspect belts and hoses
- Ensure AC is serviced
- Check tyre pressure (increases with heat)
- Carry extra water

### Driving Techniques
- Monitor temperature gauge constantly
- Use engine braking to reduce brake heat
- Allow engine to cool before shutdown
- Park in shade when possible
- Use sunshade on windshield

### Overheating Response
- Turn off AC
- Turn on heater (draws heat from engine)
- Pull over safely
- Do NOT open radiator cap hot
- Wait at least 30 minutes before checking

## High Wind Driving

### Recognition
- Trees swaying
- Debris on road
- Difficulty maintaining lane
- Wind noise increases
- Steering feels light

### Techniques
- Reduce speed
- Keep both hands on wheel (10 and 2)
- Be ready for sudden gusts
- Watch for high-profile vehicles
- Avoid passing large trucks
- Allow extra following distance

## Emergency Vehicle Handling

### Brake Failure
1. Pump brakes rapidly
2. Downshift to lower gears
3. Apply parking brake gently
4. Use emergency brake if equipped
5. Look for escape route (runaway ramp, uphill, soft shoulder)

### Steering Failure
1. Immediately slow down
2. Apply hazard lights
3. Use parking brake if needed
4. Do not continue driving
5. Call for tow truck

### Throttle Stuck
1. Shift to neutral
2. Brake firmly
3. Pull off road
4. Turn off engine when stopped
5. Do not turn key to lock (steering locks)

### Headlight Failure
1. Check switch immediately
2. Use fog lights if available
3. Turn on hazard lights
4. Slow down, pull off road
5. Do not continue without lights

## Tire Blowout

### Response
1. Grip steering wheel firmly
2. Do NOT slam brakes
3. Ease off accelerator
4. Let vehicle slow naturally
5. Steer to maintain lane
6. Signal and pull off when slow

### After Stopping
1. Activate hazard lights
2. Set reflector triangles
3. Call for assistance
4. Don't change tire on highway shoulder
5. Wait in safe location
key_points: Heavy rain: reduce speed, double following distance, use headlights
Fog: use low beams (not high), reduce speed significantly
Overheating: turn off AC, turn on heater, pull over safely
Brake failure: pump brakes, downshift, use parking brake gently
Blowout: firm grip, no brakes, let vehicle slow naturally

units.title: Fleet Management & Vehicle Care
units.content:
# Fleet Management & Vehicle Care

## Daily Fleet Operations

### Pre-Operation Checklist
- Vehicle condition inspection
- Fluid levels (oil, coolant, brake, washer)
- Tyre pressure and tread
- All lights operational
- Safety equipment present
- Documentation complete

### Post-Operation Checklist
- Refuel as needed
- Check for new damage
- Report any issues
- Clean interior and exterior
- Log odometer reading
- Secure vehicle

## Preventive Maintenance Schedule

### Daily
- Visual inspection
- Tyre pressure check
- Fluid level check
- Light operation test
- Brake test

### Weekly
- Undercarriage inspection
- Battery terminal check
- Belt and hose inspection
- Wiper blade condition
- Horn operation

### Monthly
- Brake pad thickness
- Tyre rotation
- Alignment check
- AC system check
- Exhaust system check

### Quarterly
- Coolant flush
- Brake fluid change
- Transmission service
- Fuel system cleaning
- Full diagnostic scan

### Annually
- Major service
- Manufacturer recommended maintenance
- Safety inspection
- Emission test
- Full detail

## Fuel Efficiency Management

### Driving Techniques
- Smooth acceleration
- Maintain steady speed
- Anticipate stops
- Use cruise control on highways
- Avoid excessive idling
- Remove unnecessary weight
- Keep windows closed at high speed

### Vehicle Factors
- Maintain proper tyre pressure
- Regular oil changes
- Clean air filters
- Proper wheel alignment
- Use recommended fuel grade
- Remove roof racks when not needed

### Route Optimization
- Plan efficient routes
- Avoid peak traffic
- Combine trips
- Use navigation with traffic
- Consider time of day

## Defect Reporting System

### Types of Defects
- Critical (stop immediately)
- Major (repair within 24 hours)
- Minor (schedule repair)
- Cosmetic (monitor)

### Reporting Process
1. Driver identifies defect
2. Immediate report for critical issues
3. Complete defect report form
4. Attach photos if possible
5. Management reviews
6. Schedule repair
7. Verify repair completion
8. Close report

## Vehicle Documentation

### Required Documents
- Vehicle registration
- Insurance certificate
- Road service license
- PSV badge (if applicable)
- Inspection certificate
- Maintenance logs

### Trip Documentation
- Daily log sheet
- Fuel purchase records
- Toll receipts
- Incident reports
- Passenger manifests (if PSV)

## Emergency Equipment

### Mandatory Equipment
- Fire extinguisher (serviced)
- First aid kit
- Reflector triangles (3)
- Spare tyre with tools
- Warning triangles
- Emergency contact numbers

### Recommended Equipment
- Jump start cables
- Tow rope
- Flashlight with batteries
- Basic tool kit
- Water and non-perishable food
- Blanket
- Phone charger
- Reflective vest

## Fleet Tracking & Telematics

### Benefits
- Real-time vehicle location
- Driver behavior monitoring
- Fuel efficiency tracking
- Maintenance alerts
- Route optimization
- Theft recovery

### Data Collected
- Speed and acceleration
- Braking patterns
- Idle time
- Route adherence
- Engine diagnostics
- Fuel consumption

### Driver Responsibilities
- Log into system at shift start
- Report GPS issues
- Avoid tampering with devices
- Review performance data
- Participate in coaching
key_points: Complete daily pre and post-operation inspections
Follow manufacturer preventive maintenance schedule
Report defects immediately, especially critical issues
Maintain all required vehicle documentation
Carry mandatory emergency equipment at all times

units.title: Professional Conduct & Compliance
units.content:
# Professional Conduct & Compliance

## Driver Appearance & Presentation

### Uniform Standards
- Clean, pressed uniform
- Properly fitted
- Company logos visible
- Name badge displayed
- Footwear appropriate for driving

### Personal Grooming
- Clean and neat appearance
- Good personal hygiene
- No excessive jewelry
- Hair neat and tidy
- Professional demeanor

## Code of Conduct

### Professional Behavior
- Courteous to all road users
- Patient in traffic
- No aggressive driving
- No discriminatory behavior
- Respect company property

### Prohibited Actions
- Using phone while driving
- Eating or drinking while driving
- Smoking in vehicle
- Transporting unauthorized passengers
- Carrying prohibited items
- Driving under influence

## Customer Service Standards

### Communication
- Greet customers professionally
- Speak clearly and respectfully
- Listen actively
- Confirm understanding
- Follow up on commitments

### Handling Complaints
- Listen without interrupting
- Acknowledge the issue
- Apologize for inconvenience
- Offer solution
- Escalate if needed
- Document complaint

### Difficult Situations
- Stay calm
- Don't take personally
- Focus on solution
- Know when to involve supervisor
- Maintain professionalism

## Legal Compliance

### Traffic Laws
- Obey speed limits
- Stop at all stop signs
- Yield when required
- Use signals properly
- Observe lane discipline
- No illegal U-turns

### Hours of Service
- Maximum 8 hours driving
- 30-minute break every 4 hours
- Rest between shifts
- Accurate log books
- No falsification of records

### Documentation Requirements
- Valid driving license
- Appropriate endorsements
- Medical certificate
- PSV badge (if applicable)
- Defensive driving certificate

## Company Policies

### Attendance
- Report on time
- Call before shift if late
- Follow notification procedures
- Request leave in advance
- No unauthorized absence

### Vehicle Use
- Authorized use only
- Report all accidents
- No personal use without permission
- Secure vehicle when unattended
- Report mechanical issues promptly

### Safety Policies
- Report all incidents
- Participate in safety meetings
- Follow safety procedures
- Use safety equipment
- Report unsafe conditions

## Professional Development

### Required Training
- Defensive driving refresher (every 3 years)
- First aid/CPR renewal
- Company policies updates
- New equipment training
- Regulatory changes

### Optional Development
- Advanced driving courses
- Customer service training
- Leadership skills
- Technical certifications
- Language courses

## Ethical Driving

### Environmental Responsibility
- Reduce idling
- Drive efficiently
- Report oil/fuel leaks
- Proper waste disposal
- Use recommended fuel

### Courtesy on Road
- Allow merging vehicles
- Don't block intersections
- Use turn signals
- Don't tailgate
- Yield to emergency vehicles
- Be patient with learners

### Reporting Obligations
- Accidents (immediate)
- Traffic violations (received)
- Witness to incidents
- Road hazards
- Unsafe conditions
- Suspicious activity
key_points: Maintain professional appearance and conduct at all times
Handle customer complaints calmly and professionally
Obey all traffic laws and hours of service regulations
Complete required documentation accurately
Report all incidents and safety concerns promptly

units.title: Health, Wellness & Fatigue Management
units.content:
# Health, Wellness & Fatigue Management

## Driver Health Requirements

### Medical Standards
- Valid medical examination
- Vision test passed
- No uncontrolled medical conditions
- No substance abuse
- Fit for duty certification

### Common Medical Conditions
- Diabetes: managed with regular meals
- Hypertension: medication compliance
- Sleep apnea: treatment compliance
- Epilepsy: seizure-free period
- Heart conditions: physician clearance

### Reporting Obligations
- New diagnoses
- Medication changes
- Symptoms affecting driving
- Hospitalizations
- Work-related injuries

## Fatigue Management

### Recognizing Fatigue Signs
- Frequent yawning
- Heavy eyelids
- Difficulty focusing
- Drifting from lane
- Missing exits
- Irritability
- Memory lapses
- Slower reactions

### Causes of Fatigue
- Insufficient sleep
- Long driving hours
- Night driving
- Monotonous roads
- Poor diet
- Dehydration
- Medical conditions
- Medications

### Prevention Strategies
- Get 7-9 hours sleep before driving
- Take breaks every 2 hours
- Share driving when possible
- Avoid heavy meals before driving
- Stay hydrated
- Avoid alcohol before sleep
- Maintain consistent schedule

### Break Planning
- 15-minute break every 2 hours
- 30-minute meal break every 4 hours
- Get out of vehicle during breaks
- Stretch and walk
- Healthy snacks
- Hydrate
- Rest eyes (look at distance)

### If Fatigued While Driving
- Don't push through
- Find safe place to stop
- Take 20-minute power nap
- Get fresh air
- Drink water
- Call for relief if needed
- Never drive drowsy

## Nutrition for Drivers

### Best Foods
- Complex carbohydrates (whole grains)
- Lean proteins
- Fresh fruits
- Vegetables
- Nuts and seeds
- Yogurt

### Foods to Avoid
- Heavy, greasy meals
- High sugar snacks
- Excessive caffeine
- Alcohol (before driving)
- Large portions

### Meal Planning
- Eat small, frequent meals
- Pack healthy snacks
- Plan meal stops
- Avoid eating while driving
- Stay hydrated (water)
- Limit sugary drinks

## Exercise & Physical Health

### On-the-Road Exercises
- Neck rotations
- Shoulder rolls
- Wrist stretches
- Ankle rotations
- Seated leg lifts
- Deep breathing

### Long-Term Fitness
- Regular cardio
- Strength training
- Flexibility exercises
- Back strengthening
- Core stability

### Ergonomics
- Proper seat position
- Lumbar support
- Adjustable steering wheel
- Foot positioning
- Wrist alignment
- Eye level adjustment

## Mental Health & Stress Management

### Stress Signs
- Irritability
- Difficulty concentrating
- Racing thoughts
- Physical tension
- Sleep problems
- Appetite changes

### Coping Strategies
- Deep breathing exercises
- Positive self-talk
- Listen to music/podcasts
- Take breaks
- Talk to someone
- Regular exercise
- Adequate sleep

### Work-Life Balance
- Set boundaries
- Quality time with family
- Hobbies outside work
- Adequate time off
- Vacation planning

## Substance Abuse Prevention

### Zero Tolerance
- No alcohol before or during shift
- No illegal drugs
- Prescription medication disclosure
- Over-the-counter awareness
- Random testing may occur

### Medication Awareness
- Read warnings
- Consult pharmacist
- Report to employer
- Don't drive if drowsy
- Seek alternatives

## Wellness Programs

### Company Programs
- Health screenings
- Wellness incentives
- Employee assistance program
- Smoking cessation
- Weight management

### Self-Care
- Regular check-ups
- Dental care
- Vision exams
- Mental health support
- Vaccinations
key_points: 7-9 hours sleep minimum before driving
Take 15-minute break every 2 hours
Never drive if fatigued - take power nap
Eat small, frequent healthy meals
Report any medical condition affecting driving

units.title: Accident Investigation & Reporting
units.content:
# Accident Investigation & Reporting

## Immediate Response

### At the Scene
1. Stop immediately
2. Check for injuries
3. Call emergency services (999)
4. Set warning triangles
5. Move to safe area if possible
6. Do not admit fault
7. Exchange information
8. Take photos
9. Get witness information
10. Report to police

### Passenger/Injured Persons
- Do not move injured unless immediate danger
- Provide first aid if trained
- Keep injured warm
- Reassure calmly
- Wait for ambulance

## Information Gathering

### Required Information
- Date, time, location
- Weather conditions
- Road conditions
- Vehicle positions
- Damage assessment
- Injuries sustained
- Other party details:
  - Name, address, phone
  - License number
  - Insurance information
  - Vehicle registration
  - Driver's license number

### Photographs to Take
- Overall scene
- Vehicle positions
- Damage to all vehicles
- Skid marks
- Road conditions
- Traffic signs/signals
- Injuries
- License plates
- Driver's licenses
- Insurance documents

### Witness Information
- Names
- Contact information
- Statements (written if possible)
- Location at time
- What they observed
- Willingness to testify

## Police Reporting

### When to Call Police
- Any injury accident
- Significant damage
- Hit and run
- Suspected DUI
- Disputed fault
- Commercial vehicle involved
- Hazardous materials

### Information for Police
- Your account of events
- Other party's account
- Witness statements
- Photographs
- Diagram of scene
- Your license and documents

### Obtaining Report
- Get police incident number
- Request copy of report
- Follow up if needed
- Provide additional information
- Keep copy for records

## Company Reporting

### Immediate Notification
- Contact dispatch/supervisor
- Provide preliminary information
- Follow company protocol
- Don't discuss fault
- Await instructions

### Written Report
- Complete company accident report
- Detailed narrative
- Attach photographs
- Include witness statements
- Diagram of scene
- Submit within required timeframe

## Insurance Procedures

### Notification
- Report to insurer promptly
- Provide police report number
- Share other party information
- Don't admit fault
- Follow their instructions

### Claims Process
- Claim number assigned
- Adjuster contact
- Vehicle inspection
- Repair authorization
- Rental vehicle if needed

### Documentation
- Keep all correspondence
- Save all receipts
- Track claim status
- Report delays
- Document all communication

## Preventative Measures

### Learning from Accidents
- Root cause analysis
- Identify contributing factors
- Driver retraining if needed
- Policy review
- Procedure updates

### Safety Improvements
- Additional training
- Vehicle modifications
- Route changes
- Schedule adjustments
- Equipment updates

## Legal Considerations

### Do Not
- Admit fault
- Sign anything without review
- Discuss settlement
- Post on social media
- Destroy evidence

### Do
- Cooperate with police
- Provide factual information
- Seek legal advice if needed
- Maintain confidentiality
- Preserve evidence

## Return to Duty

### Assessment
- Medical clearance
- Psychological evaluation (if needed)
- Additional training required
- Supervisor review
- Return to duty plan

### Support
- Counseling if needed
- Mentoring program
- Graduated return
- Performance monitoring
- Follow-up evaluation
key_points: Call emergency services first if injuries
Take photos of everything at scene
Get witness information and statements
Report to company immediately
Never admit fault at scene
